content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: kvcache-ai/ktransformers
项目名: ktransformers
讲义目录: ktransformers-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/kvcache-ai/ktransformers/blob/cb9f47d142a507cac5d74450b30463d2e8d1cf58/
当前 HEAD: cb9f47d142a507cac5d74450b30463d2e8d1cf58
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "ktransformers",
  "rationale": "本项目是面向 MoE 大模型的 CPU-GPU 异构推理/微调框架，且仓库已重组：根目录的 ktransformers 是轻量 shim 包，真实运行时在 kt-kernel/，旧一体化框架归档于 archive/。因此学习路线以 kt-kernel 为核心，按\"先认知、再构建、后用 CLI、再读 Python API、再进后端与集成、最后下探 C++/CUDA/SFT 内核与测试\"的顺序展开三层。入门层（单元1-3）帮读者建立项目定位、仓库结构、构建安装与 kt CLI 使用；进阶层（单元4-7）讲清 KTMoEWrapper 工厂、CPUInfer 引擎、四类后端、权重加载、SGLang 集成、专家调度与权重量化；专家层（单元8-11）下探 pybind 绑定、AMX/AVX/llamafile/moe_kernel/kvcache 算子、CUDA 路径、SFT 训练子系统及性能测试与二次开发。单元划分对应真实目录边界，讲义间通过 depends_on 形成 DAG，每篇只解决一个主题并配真实源码与实践任务。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "项目认知与仓库结构",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "KTransformers 项目定位与整体架构",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲清 KTransformers 是什么：面向 MoE 大模型的 CPU-GPU 异构推理与微调框架，以及推理（kt-kernel）与微调（SFT）两条主线。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 CPU-GPU 异构推理（热专家在 GPU、冷专家在 CPU）的核心思想",
            "区分 kt-kernel 推理与 LLaMA-Factory 集成 SFT 两条能力",
            "了解项目维护方与应用场景"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "README_ZH.md",
            "kt-kernel/README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "项目概述",
            "能力矩阵",
            "性能示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README，用自己的话写出 KTransformers 解决的三个核心问题，并列举它支持的两类用户能力及各自入口文件。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库目录结构与代码组织",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-repository-structure.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "梳理仓库顶层与 kt-kernel 子目录结构，说明 archive/ 与 kt-kernel/ 的关系，定位 python/、operators/、cpu_backend/、cuda/、scripts/ 等核心目录。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握仓库顶层与 kt-kernel 内部目录布局",
            "理解为何旧框架归档到 archive/，新代码集中在 kt-kernel/",
            "能快速定位推理内核、CLI、SFT、构建脚本所在位置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "kt-kernel/README.md",
            "kt-kernel/pyproject.toml"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "顶层目录",
            "kt-kernel 子目录",
            "归档目录说明"
          ],
          "practice_task": "绘制一张仓库目录树（到二级），标注每个目录的职责，并指出推理 Python 包、C++ 算子、CUDA 算子分别位于何处。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "顶层 shim 包与安装入口",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-top-level-package.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解根目录 ktransformers.py 轻量 shim、setup.py 的 extras（[sft]、[sglang]）、version.py 与 pyproject.toml 如何把安装转发到 kt-kernel。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 pip install ktransformers 实际安装的是 kt-kernel",
            "掌握 [sft] 与 [sglang] 两个 extras 的作用",
            "了解 has_sft_support() 的检测逻辑"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "ktransformers.py",
            "setup.py",
            "pyproject.toml",
            "version.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "shim 模块",
            "extras 依赖",
            "版本管理"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在本地执行 python -c \"import ktransformers; print(ktransformers.__version__, ktransformers.has_sft_support())\"，记录输出并解释每个值的来源。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1",
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "构建与安装",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "安装方式总览",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-installation-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "对比 PyPI 预编译 wheel 与源码编译两种安装路径，介绍根目录 install.sh 与 kt-kernel/install.sh、autosetup.sh 的分工。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "区分 pip install kt-kernel 与源码 ./install.sh 的适用场景",
            "理解 wheel 内置多 CPU 变体与静态 CUDA 运行时",
            "掌握安装后用 kt version 与 import kt_kernel 验证的方法"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "install.sh",
            "kt-kernel/install.sh",
            "kt-kernel/autosetup.sh",
            "kt-kernel/README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "PyPI 安装",
            "源码安装",
            "验证步骤"
          ],
          "practice_task": "按 README 用 pip 安装 kt-kernel，运行 kt version 与 import kt_kernel 打印 __cpu_variant__ 与 __version__，截图或记录结果。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "构建系统与 CPU 指令集配置",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 CMakeLists.txt 的构建选项（CUDA/AMX/KML/MOE_KERNEL 等）、CPUINFER_* 环境变量与 cmake/DetectCPU.cmake 的指令集检测。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 KTRANSFORMERS_USE_CUDA、KTRANSFORMERS_CPU_USE_AMX 等关键开关",
            "理解 CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT（NATIVE/AVX512/AVX2/FANCY）与多变体构建",
            "了解 DetectCPU.cmake 如何从 /proc/cpuinfo 自动探测能力"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "kt-kernel/CMakeLists.txt",
            "kt-kernel/CMakePresets.json",
            "kt-kernel/cmake/DetectCPU.cmake",
            "kt-kernel/install.sh"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "CMake 选项",
            "指令集检测",
            "环境变量"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 --manual 模式配置一次 AVX2+AMX=OFF 的可分发构建，列出你设置的环境变量并解释每个的作用。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1",
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "运行时 CPU 变体检测与加载",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-cpu-variant-detect.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 python/_cpu_detect.py 如何在 import 时探测 CPU 特性、选择最优 .so 变体并支持回退链与 KT_KERNEL_CPU_VARIANT 覆盖。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 amx/avx512_bf16/.../avx2 变体的优先级与判定",
            "掌握多变体 .so 与单变体 .so 的加载与回退链",
            "会用 KT_KERNEL_DEBUG 与 KT_KERNEL_CPU_VARIANT 排查加载问题"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "kt-kernel/python/_cpu_detect.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/__init__.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "特性探测",
            "扩展加载",
            "回退链"
          ],
          "practice_task": "设置 KT_KERNEL_DEBUG=1 后 import kt_kernel，观察打印的检测与加载过程；再强制 KT_KERNEL_CPU_VARIANT=avx2 重复一次，对比差异。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "KT CLI 入门",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "KT CLI 总览与入口结构",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-cli-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 kt 命令行工具的整体结构：typer 框架、console-script 入口、子命令注册、首运行向导与 i18n。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 kt run/chat/model/doctor/config/version 等子命令用途",
            "理解 pyproject.toml 中 kt 入点与 cli/main.py 的关系",
            "了解 i18n 与 shell 补全机制"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/main.py",
            "kt-kernel/pyproject.toml",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/i18n.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "入口与子命令",
            "首运行向导",
            "国际化"
          ],
          "practice_task": "运行 kt --help 与 kt version，列出所有子命令，并用 kt config init 走一遍首运行向导。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "用 kt run 启动推理服务",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-kt-run-launch.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 commands/run.py 如何把模型名解析为路径、交互式收集参数，并拼装 python -m sglang.launch_server 命令启动 CPU-GPU 异构推理。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 kt run 从模型名到 sglang 启动命令的完整流程",
            "掌握 _build_sglang_command 注入的 kt-* 参数",
            "了解交互式配置与未知参数透传机制"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/commands/run.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/utils/run_interactive.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/utils/sglang_checker.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模型解析",
            "参数收集",
            "命令拼装"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 kt run 选择一个已注册或本地模型（可先用 --help 查看选项），打印最终将执行的 sglang 启动命令（不真正启动大模型也可只观察命令构造）。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l1",
            "u2-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l3",
          "title": "模型管理与配置",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u3-l3-model-config-mgmt.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 commands/model.py 的用户模型注册表、scan/add/edit/verify/download，以及 commands/config.py 与 config/settings.py 的配置文件管理。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 user_models.yaml 注册表与内置 ModelRegistry 的区别",
            "掌握 kt model scan/add/list 与 kt config show/set 的用法",
            "了解配置文件 ~/.ktransformers/config.yaml 的结构"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/commands/model.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/commands/config.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/config/settings.py",
            "kt-kernel/python/cli/utils/user_model_registry.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模型注册表",
            "配置管理",
            "内置模型库"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 kt model add 登记一个本地模型目录，再 kt config set 修改一项服务器配置并 kt config show 查看结果。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u4",
      "title": "Python 推理 API",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 4,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u4-l1",
          "title": "KTMoEWrapper 工厂与后端分发",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u4-l1-ktmoewrapper-factory.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 experts.py 中 KTMoEWrapper 作为工厂接口，依据 mode 与 method 分发到 AMX/Native/Llamafile/General 后端，并列出推理与 SFT 方法集合。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 KTMoEWrapper.__new__ 的工厂分发逻辑",
            "掌握 INFERENCE_METHODS 与 SFT_METHODS 的分类",
            "了解 method 到 backend_cls 的映射关系"
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 KTransformers 项目定位与整体架构

KTransformers 是一个用 CPU-GPU 异构计算高效推理和微调超大 MoE 模型的研究框架，由清华 MADSys 实验室、Approaching.AI、9#AISoft 等维护。其核心思想是按专家热度分工：MoE 模型中被频繁激活的热专家放 GPU（显存小但快），冷专家放 CPU（内存大但慢），并由 kt-kernel 提供 AMX/AVX 优化的 CPU MoE 算子，从而在有限显存加大容量内存的机器上跑起原本装不下的数百 B 级模型。本讲覆盖三个最小模块：项目概述、能力矩阵、性能示例。对外暴露两条共享底层算子却相互独立的能力——推理（入口 kt-kernel/README.md，配合 kvcache-ai 的 sglang-kt fork）与微调 SFT（入口 doc/en/SFT 快速上手，配合 LLaMA-Factory）。关键术语包括：MoE 专家混合、热/冷专家、量化、AMX/AVX、SGLang、LLaMA-Factory。仓库已重组：旧一体化框架归档到 archive/，新运行时集中在 kt-kernel/；顶层 ktransformers.py 是转发到 kt-kernel 的轻量 shim，SFT 为可选能力，由 has_sft_support() 通过尝试导入 kt_kernel.sft 检测。性能示例（如 DeepSeek-R1 在 8×L20 上推理、DeepSeek-V3 在 4×4090 上微调）展示了在亲民硬件上跑超大 MoE 的核心价值。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构与代码组织

本讲拆解 KTransformers 仓库的目录结构与代码组织，分三个最小模块：顶层目录、`kt-kernel` 子目录、归档目录 `archive/`。核心结论：顶层是「文档 + shim + 运行时子项目」的组合，运行时代码全部集中在 `kt-kernel/`；顶层的 `ktransformers.py`、`setup.py` 只是门面，通过 `install_requires` 转发安装 `kt-kernel`，故 `pip install ktransformers` 实际装的是 `kt-kernel`。`kt-kernel/` 内部按语言分层：Python 包在 `python/`、C++ CPU 算子在 `operators/`、CUDA GPU 算子在 `cuda/`、CPU 线程运行时在 `cpu_backend/`，C++↔Python 桥梁为 `ext_bindings.cpp`；微调（SFT）在 `python/sft/`，`kt` 命令在 `python/cli/`。关键术语：shim（垫片）、`package-dir` 包目录映射、`kt-kernel`（发布名/连字符）与 `kt_kernel`（导入名/下划线）的区分。`archive/` 是旧一体化框架，已不参与新流程，遇其路径需谨慎参考。三类代码快速定位：推理 Python 包 → `python/`，C++ 算子 → `operators/`，CUDA 算子 → `cuda/`。
### u1-l3 顶层 shim 包与安装入口

本讲聚焦仓库根目录的「门面」层，解释 `pip install ktransformers` 真正安装的是什么。核心结论：顶层 `ktransformers` 是一个 shim（垫片）包，自身几乎不含逻辑，通过 `setup.py` 的 `install_requires=[f"kt-kernel=={_v}"]` 将安装请求转发给真正的运行时 `kt-kernel`，二者以严格相等的版本号锁死对齐。讲义覆盖三个最小模块：shim 模块 `ktransformers.py` 提供 `__version__`（优先读 `importlib.metadata`，回退读 `version.py`）与惰性探测函数 `has_sft_support()`（尝试 `import kt_kernel.sft`，成功即代表 SFT 可用）；extras 依赖中 `[sft]` 拉取 `transformers-kt`、`accelerate-kt`，`[sglang]` 拉取 `sglang-kt`；版本管理遵循单源真相，全仓库仅 `version.py` 一处定义版本号，被顶层 `setup.py`、`ktransformers.py` 与 `kt-kernel/setup.py` 三处共享读取。关键术语：shim 包、发布名与导入名（`kt-kernel`/`kt_kernel`）、extras、`importlib.metadata`、单源真相、`py-modules`。
### u2-l1 安装方式总览

本讲总览 kt-kernel 两条安装路径：`pip install kt-kernel` 的 PyPI 预编译 wheel（官方推荐、无需编译工具链），以及 `./install.sh` 源码编译（用于 AMD/ARM 后端、自定义 CUDA、可移植二进制或本机极致优化）。官方 wheel 有两大省心特性：一个包内置多个 CPU 变体（AMX/AVX512 各子集/AVX2），变体选择推迟到 import 时按本机指令集自动完成；静态 CUDA 运行时，无需装 toolkit、无 GPU 时自动禁用。源码安装分两层：根目录 install.sh 作编排者，串联 submodule→deps→sglang→kt-kernel；kt-kernel/install.sh 作构建者，含自动探测（-march=native）与 --manual 两模式。安装后用 `kt version` 与 `import kt_kernel` 双重验证，读取 `__version__`、`__cpu_variant__`（import 时动态探测）并判断 CUDA，异构推理须确认装的是 sglang-kt fork 而非官方 sglang。`__cpu_variant__` 运行时动态决定、`__version__` 优先取包元数据再回退 version.py，体现单源真相与运行时自适应设计，其检测细节留给后续 u2-l2、u2-l3。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u2-l2
- 文件名: u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md
- 写入路径: ktransformers-tutorial/u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md
- 标题: 构建系统与 CPU 指令集配置
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 讲解 CMakeLists.txt 的构建选项（CUDA/AMX/KML/MOE_KERNEL 等）、CPUINFER_* 环境变量与 cmake/DetectCPU.cmake 的指令集检测。
- 学习目标: 掌握 KTRANSFORMERS_USE_CUDA、KTRANSFORMERS_CPU_USE_AMX 等关键开关；理解 CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT（NATIVE/AVX512/AVX2/FANCY）与多变体构建；了解 DetectCPU.cmake 如何从 /proc/cpuinfo 自动探测能力
- 应覆盖的最小模块: CMake 选项，指令集检测，环境变量
- 关键源码: kt-kernel/CMakeLists.txt，kt-kernel/CMakePresets.json，kt-kernel/cmake/DetectCPU.cmake，kt-kernel/install.sh
- 代码实践任务: 用 --manual 模式配置一次 AVX2+AMX=OFF 的可分发构建，列出你设置的环境变量并解释每个的作用。
- 依赖讲义: u2-l1，u1-l2

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `ktransformers-tutorial/u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 KTransformers 项目定位与整体架构

KTransformers 是一个用 CPU-GPU 异构计算高效推理和微调超大 MoE 模型的研究框架，由清华 MADSys 实验室、Approaching.AI、9#AISoft 等维护。其核心思想是按专家热度分工：MoE 模型中被频繁激活的热专家放 GPU（显存小但快），冷专家放 CPU（内存大但慢），并由 kt-kernel 提供 AMX/AVX 优化的 CPU MoE 算子，从而在有限显存加大容量内存的机器上跑起原本装不下的数百 B 级模型。本讲覆盖三个最小模块：项目概述、能力矩阵、性能示例。对外暴露两条共享底层算子却相互独立的能力——推理（入口 kt-kernel/README.md，配合 kvcache-ai 的 sglang-kt fork）与微调 SFT（入口 doc/en/SFT 快速上手，配合 LLaMA-Factory）。关键术语包括：MoE 专家混合、热/冷专家、量化、AMX/AVX、SGLang、LLaMA-Factory。仓库已重组：旧一体化框架归档到 archive/，新运行时集中在 kt-kernel/；顶层 ktransformers.py 是转发到 kt-kernel 的轻量 shim，SFT 为可选能力，由 has_sft_support() 通过尝试导入 kt_kernel.sft 检测。性能示例（如 DeepSeek-R1 在 8×L20 上推理、DeepSeek-V3 在 4×4090 上微调）展示了在亲民硬件上跑超大 MoE 的核心价值。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构与代码组织

本讲拆解 KTransformers 仓库的目录结构与代码组织，分三个最小模块：顶层目录、`kt-kernel` 子目录、归档目录 `archive/`。核心结论：顶层是「文档 + shim + 运行时子项目」的组合，运行时代码全部集中在 `kt-kernel/`；顶层的 `ktransformers.py`、`setup.py` 只是门面，通过 `install_requires` 转发安装 `kt-kernel`，故 `pip install ktransformers` 实际装的是 `kt-kernel`。`kt-kernel/` 内部按语言分层：Python 包在 `python/`、C++ CPU 算子在 `operators/`、CUDA GPU 算子在 `cuda/`、CPU 线程运行时在 `cpu_backend/`，C++↔Python 桥梁为 `ext_bindings.cpp`；微调（SFT）在 `python/sft/`，`kt` 命令在 `python/cli/`。关键术语：shim（垫片）、`package-dir` 包目录映射、`kt-kernel`（发布名/连字符）与 `kt_kernel`（导入名/下划线）的区分。`archive/` 是旧一体化框架，已不参与新流程，遇其路径需谨慎参考。三类代码快速定位：推理 Python 包 → `python/`，C++ 算子 → `operators/`，CUDA 算子 → `cuda/`。
### u1-l3 顶层 shim 包与安装入口

本讲聚焦仓库根目录的「门面」层，解释 `pip install ktransformers` 真正安装的是什么。核心结论：顶层 `ktransformers` 是一个 shim（垫片）包，自身几乎不含逻辑，通过 `setup.py` 的 `install_requires=[f"kt-kernel=={_v}"]` 将安装请求转发给真正的运行时 `kt-kernel`，二者以严格相等的版本号锁死对齐。讲义覆盖三个最小模块：shim 模块 `ktransformers.py` 提供 `__version__`（优先读 `importlib.metadata`，回退读 `version.py`）与惰性探测函数 `has_sft_support()`（尝试 `import kt_kernel.sft`，成功即代表 SFT 可用）；extras 依赖中 `[sft]` 拉取 `transformers-kt`、`accelerate-kt`，`[sglang]` 拉取 `sglang-kt`；版本管理遵循单源真相，全仓库仅 `version.py` 一处定义版本号，被顶层 `setup.py`、`ktransformers.py` 与 `kt-kernel/setup.py` 三处共享读取。关键术语：shim 包、发布名与导入名（`kt-kernel`/`kt_kernel`）、extras、`importlib.metadata`、单源真相、`py-modules`。
### u2-l1 安装方式总览

本讲总览 kt-kernel 两条安装路径：`pip install kt-kernel` 的 PyPI 预编译 wheel（官方推荐、无需编译工具链），以及 `./install.sh` 源码编译（用于 AMD/ARM 后端、自定义 CUDA、可移植二进制或本机极致优化）。官方 wheel 有两大省心特性：一个包内置多个 CPU 变体（AMX/AVX512 各子集/AVX2），变体选择推迟到 import 时按本机指令集自动完成；静态 CUDA 运行时，无需装 toolkit、无 GPU 时自动禁用。源码安装分两层：根目录 install.sh 作编排者，串联 submodule→deps→sglang→kt-kernel；kt-kernel/install.sh 作构建者，含自动探测（-march=native）与 --manual 两模式。安装后用 `kt version` 与 `import kt_kernel` 双重验证，读取 `__version__`、`__cpu_variant__`（import 时动态探测）并判断 CUDA，异构推理须确认装的是 sglang-kt fork 而非官方 sglang。`__cpu_variant__` 运行时动态决定、`__version__` 优先取包元数据再回退 version.py，体现单源真相与运行时自适应设计，其检测细节留给后续 u2-l2、u2-l3。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u2-l2
- 文件名: u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md
- 写入路径: ktransformers-tutorial/u2-l2-build-cmake-cpu.md
- 标题: 构建系统与 CPU 指令集配置
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 讲解 CMakeLists.txt 的构建选项（CUDA/AMX/KML/MOE_KERNEL 等）、CPUINFER_* 环境变量与 cmake/DetectCPU.cmake 的指令集检测。
- 学习目标: 掌握 KTRANSFORMERS_USE_CUDA、KTRANSFORMERS_CPU_USE_AMX 等关键开关；理解 CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT（NATIVE/AVX512/AVX2/FANCY）与多变体构建；了解 DetectCPU.cmake 如何从 /proc/cpuinfo 自动探测能力
- 应覆盖的最小模块: CMake 选项，指令集检测，环境变量
- 关键源码: kt-kernel/CMakeLists.txt，kt-kernel/CMakePresets.json，kt-kernel/cmake/DetectCPU.cmake，kt-kernel/install.sh
- 代码实践任务: 用 --manual 模式配置一次 AVX2+AMX=OFF 的可分发构建，列出你设置的环境变量并解释每个的作用。
- 依赖讲义: u2-l1，u1-l2

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

# CPU Feature Detection for kt-kernel
# Detects CPU capabilities and sets appropriate compiler flags

function(detect_cpu_features)
    set(HAS_AVX2 OFF PARENT_SCOPE)
    set(HAS_AVX512F OFF PARENT_SCOPE)
    set(HAS_AVX512_VNNI OFF PARENT_SCOPE)
    set(HAS_AVX512_BF16 OFF PARENT_SCOPE)
    set(HAS_AVX512_VBMI OFF PARENT_SCOPE)
    set(HAS_AMX OFF PARENT_SCOPE)

    if(NOT EXISTS "/proc/cpuinfo")
        message(STATUS "CPU detection: /proc/cpuinfo not found, skipping auto-detection")
        return()
    endif()

    # Read CPU flags from /proc/cpuinfo
    file(READ "/proc/cpuinfo" CPUINFO_CONTENT)
    string(REGEX MATCH "flags[ \t]*:[ \t]*([^\n]*)" FLAGS_LINE "${CPUINFO_CONTENT}")
    if(NOT CMAKE_MATCH_1)
        message(STATUS "CPU detection: Could not parse CPU flags")
        return()
    endif()

    set(CPU_FLAGS "${CMAKE_MATCH_1}")
    string(REPLACE " " ";" CPU_FLAGS_LIST "${CPU_FLAGS}")

    # Check for each feature
    if("avx2" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
        set(HAS_AVX2 ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    if("avx512f" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
        set(HAS_AVX512F ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    if("avx512_vnni" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST OR "avx512vnni" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
        set(HAS_AVX512_VNNI ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    if("avx512_bf16" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST OR "avx512bf16" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
        set(HAS_AVX512_BF16 ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    if("avx512_vbmi" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST OR "avx512vbmi" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
        set(HAS_AVX512_VBMI ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    # Check for AMX (need all three)
    set(AMX_COUNT 0)
    foreach(flag "amx_tile" "amx_int8" "amx_bf16")
        if("${flag}" IN_LIST CPU_FLAGS_LIST)
            math(EXPR AMX_COUNT "${AMX_COUNT} + 1")
        endif()
    endforeach()
    if(AMX_COUNT EQUAL 3)
        set(HAS_AMX ON PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()

    # Get CPU model name for display
    string(REGEX MATCH "model name[ \t]*:[ \t]*([^\n]*)" MODEL_LINE "${CPUINFO_CONTENT}")
    if(CMAKE_MATCH_1)
        set(CPU_MODEL "${CMAKE_MATCH_1}" PARENT_SCOPE)
    endif()
endfunction()

# Main detection and configuration
message(STATUS "")
message(STATUS "========================================")
message(STATUS "CPU Feature Detection (CMake)")
message(STATUS "========================================")

# Check if variables were already set by install.sh/setup.py
set(FROM_INSTALL_SH OFF)
if(DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_VNNI OR DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_BF16 OR DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_VBMI)
    set(FROM_INSTALL_SH ON)
    message(STATUS "Detected configuration from install.sh/setup.py")
    message(STATUS "  LLAMA_AVX512:      ${LLAMA_AVX512}")
    message(STATUS "  LLAMA_AVX512_VNNI: ${LLAMA_AVX512_VNNI}")
    message(STATUS "  LLAMA_AVX512_BF16: ${LLAMA_AVX512_BF16}")
    message(STATUS "  LLAMA_AVX512_VBMI: ${LLAMA_AVX512_VBMI}")
    message(STATUS "")
    message(STATUS "Skipping auto-detection (using install.sh settings)")
    message(STATUS "========================================")
    message(STATUS "")
    return()
endif()

# Detect CPU features (only if not set by install.sh)
detect_cpu_features()

if(CPU_MODEL)
    message(STATUS "CPU Model: ${CPU_MODEL}")
endif()

message(STATUS "")
message(STATUS "Detected features:")
message(STATUS "  AVX2:         ${HAS_AVX2}")
message(STATUS "  AVX512F:      ${HAS_AVX512F}")
message(STATUS "  AVX512_VNNI:  ${HAS_AVX512_VNNI}")
message(STATUS "  AVX512_BF16:  ${HAS_AVX512_BF16}")
message(STATUS "  AVX512_VBMI:  ${HAS_AVX512_VBMI}")
message(STATUS "  AMX:          ${HAS_AMX}")
message(STATUS "")

# Auto-enable features based on detection
# Only set if not already defined by user via -D flags
if(NOT DEFINED LLAMA_AVX2 AND HAS_AVX2)
    set(LLAMA_AVX2 ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AVX2" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AVX2")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512 AND HAS_AVX512F)
    set(LLAMA_AVX512 ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AVX512F" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AVX512F")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_VNNI AND HAS_AVX512_VNNI)
    set(LLAMA_AVX512_VNNI ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AVX512_VNNI" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AVX512_VNNI")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_BF16 AND HAS_AVX512_BF16)
    set(LLAMA_AVX512_BF16 ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AVX512_BF16" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AVX512_BF16")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED LLAMA_AVX512_VBMI AND HAS_AVX512_VBMI)
    set(LLAMA_AVX512_VBMI ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AVX512_VBMI" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AVX512_VBMI")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED KTRANSFORMERS_CPU_USE_AMX AND HAS_AMX)
    set(KTRANSFORMERS_CPU_USE_AMX ON CACHE BOOL "Enable AMX" FORCE)
    message(STATUS "Auto-enabled: AMX")
endif()

message(STATUS "")
message(STATUS "Note: You can override by passing -DLLAMA_AVX512_BF16=OFF etc.")
message(STATUS "Note: Or use install.sh with environment variables")
message(STATUS "========================================")
message(STATUS "")

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	usage() {
5	  cat <<EOF
6	Usage: $0 [SUBCOMMAND] [BUILD_OPTIONS]
7	
8	Two-step installation in one file. Choose a subcommand:
9	
10	SUBCOMMANDS:
11	  deps            Install system prerequisites only
12	  build           Build and install kt-kernel (no dependency install)
13	  all             Run deps then build (default when no subcommand)
14	  -h, --help      Show this help message
15	
16	BUILD_OPTIONS (for "build" or "all"):
17	  (none)          Auto-detect CPU and configure automatically (recommended)
18	  --manual        Skip auto-detection, use manual configuration (see below)
19	  --no-clean      Do not delete local build/ before building (default cleans)
20	
21	AUTO-DETECTION (Default):
22	  The script will automatically detect your CPU and use ALL available features:
23	  - CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT = NATIVE (uses -march=native)
24	  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX   = ON/OFF (based on detection)
25	  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI = ON/OFF (with fallback if OFF)
26	  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16 = ON/OFF (with fallback if OFF)
27	  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI = ON/OFF (required for FP8 MoE)
28	
29	  ✓ Best performance on YOUR machine
30	  ✗ Binary may NOT work on different/older CPUs
31	
32	  Use this when: Installing for local use only
33	
34	MANUAL CONFIGURATION:
35	  Use --manual flag when building for DISTRIBUTION or different machines.
36	  Set these environment variables before running:
37	
38	  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT   - Target CPU instruction set
39	                            Options: AVX512, AVX2, FANCY, NATIVE
40	  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX     - Enable Intel AMX support
41	                            Options: ON, OFF
42	
43	Distribution examples (portable binaries):
44	
45	┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
46	│ Configuration          │ Target CPUs              │ Use Case             │
47	├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
48	│ AVX512 + AMX=OFF       │ Skylake-X, Ice Lake,     │ General distribution │
49	│                        │ Cascade Lake, Zen 4      │ (recommended)        │
50	├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
51	│ AVX2 + AMX=OFF         │ Haswell (2013) and newer │ Maximum compatibility│
52	├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
53	│ FANCY + AMX=OFF        │ Ice Lake+, Zen 4+        │ Modern CPUs only     │
54	│                        │ (with full AVX512 ext)   │                      │
55	└────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
56	
57	  Use this when: Building Docker images, PyPI packages, or deploying to clusters
58	
59	  Example: Build for general distribution
60	    export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512
61	    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
62	    $0 build --manual
63	    # Result: Works on any CPU with AVX512 (2017+)
64	
65	  Example: Build for maximum compatibility
66	    export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX2
67	    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
68	    $0 build --manual
69	    # Result: Works on any CPU with AVX2 (2013+)
70	
71	Optional variables (with defaults):
72	  CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE=Release           Build type (Debug/RelWithDebInfo/Release)
73	  CPUINFER_PARALLEL=8                   Number of parallel build jobs
74	  CPUINFER_VERBOSE=1                    Verbose build output (0/1)
75	  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=ON/OFF    Override VNNI detection (auto if unset)
76	  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=ON/OFF    Override BF16 detection (auto if unset)
77	  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=ON/OFF    Override VBMI detection (auto if unset)
78	  CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE=ON/OFF       Enable native crash tracing (default OFF)
79	
80	Software Fallback Support:
81	  ✓ If VNNI not available: Uses AVX512BW fallback (2-3x slower but works)
82	  ✓ If BF16 not available: Uses AVX512F fallback (5-10x slower but works)
83	  → Old CPUs with only AVX512F+BW can run all code (slower but functional)
84	
85	EOF
86	  exit 1
87	}
88	
89	install_dependencies() {
90	  echo "Checking and installing system dependencies..."
91	
92	  # Determine if we need to use sudo
93	  SUDO=""
94	  if [ "${EUID:-0}" -ne 0 ]; then
95	    if command -v sudo &> /dev/null; then
96	      SUDO="sudo"
97	    else
98	      echo "Warning: Not running as root and sudo not found. Package installation may fail."
99	      echo "Please run as root or install sudo."
100	    fi
101	  fi
102	
103	  if command -v conda &> /dev/null; then
104	    echo "Installing cmake via conda..."
105	    conda install -y cmake
106	  else
107	    echo "Warning: conda not found. Skipping cmake installation via conda."
108	    echo "Please install conda or manually install cmake."
109	  fi
110	
111	  # Detect OS type
112	  if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then
113	    . /etc/os-release
114	    OS=$ID
115	  elif [ -f /etc/debian_version ]; then
116	    OS="debian"
117	  elif [ -f /etc/redhat-release ]; then
118	    OS="rhel"
119	  else
120	    echo "Warning: Unable to detect OS type. Skipping dependency installation."
121	    return 0
122	  fi
123	
124	  # Install dependencies based on OS
125	  case "$OS" in
126	    debian|ubuntu|linuxmint|pop)
127	      echo "Detected Debian-based system. Installing libhwloc-dev and pkg-config..."
128	      $SUDO apt update
129	      $SUDO apt install -y libhwloc-dev pkg-config
130	      ;;
131	    fedora|rhel|centos|rocky|almalinux)
132	      echo "Detected Red Hat-based system. Installing hwloc-devel and pkgconfig..."
133	      $SUDO dnf install -y hwloc-devel pkgconfig || $SUDO yum install -y hwloc-devel pkgconfig
134	      ;;
135	    arch|manjaro)
136	      echo "Detected Arch-based system. Installing hwloc and pkgconf..."
137	      $SUDO pacman -S --noconfirm hwloc pkgconf
138	      ;;
139	    opensuse*|sles)
140	      echo "Detected openSUSE-based system. Installing hwloc-devel and pkg-config..."
141	      $SUDO zypper install -y hwloc-devel pkg-config
142	      ;;
143	    *)
144	      echo "Warning: Unsupported OS '$OS'. Please manually install libhwloc-dev and pkg-config."
145	      ;;
146	  esac
147	}
148	
149	# Function to detect CPU features
150	# Returns: "has_amx has_avx512f has_avx512_vnni has_avx512_bf16 has_avx512_vbmi" (space-separated 0/1 values)
151	detect_cpu_features() {
152	  local has_amx=0
153	  local has_avx512f=0
154	  local has_avx512_vnni=0
155	  local has_avx512_bf16=0
156	  local has_avx512_vbmi=0
157	
158	  if [ -f /proc/cpuinfo ]; then
159	    local cpu_flags
160	    cpu_flags=$(grep -m1 "^flags" /proc/cpuinfo | tr ' ' '\n')
161	
162	    # Check for AMX support on Linux
163	    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "amx_tile|amx_int8|amx_bf16"; then
164	      has_amx=1
165	    fi
166	
167	    # Check for AVX512F (foundation)
168	    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512f"; then
169	      has_avx512f=1
170	    fi
171	
172	    # Check for AVX512_VNNI support
173	    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_vnni|avx512vnni"; then
174	      has_avx512_vnni=1
175	    fi
176	
177	    # Check for AVX512_BF16 support
178	    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_bf16|avx512bf16"; then
179	      has_avx512_bf16=1
180	    fi
181	
182	    # Check for AVX512_VBMI support
183	    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_vbmi|avx512vbmi"; then
184	      has_avx512_vbmi=1
185	    fi
186	  elif [ "$(uname)" = "Darwin" ]; then
187	    # macOS doesn't have AMX (ARM or Intel without AMX)
188	    has_amx=0
189	    has_avx512f=0
190	    has_avx512_vnni=0
191	    has_avx512_bf16=0
192	    has_avx512_vbmi=0
193	  fi
194	
195	  echo "$has_amx $has_avx512f $has_avx512_vnni $has_avx512_bf16 $has_avx512_vbmi"
196	}
197	
198	build_step() {
199	  # Parse build-only flags from arguments to this function
200	  local MANUAL_MODE=0
201	  local CLEAN_BUILD=1
202	  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
203	    case "$1" in
204	      --manual) MANUAL_MODE=1; shift ;;
205	      --no-clean) CLEAN_BUILD=0; shift ;;
206	      -h|--help) usage ;;
207	      *) break ;;
208	    esac
209	  done
210	
211	  # Clean local build directory to ensure a fresh CMake/configure
212	  local REPO_ROOT
213	  REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
214	  if [[ "$CLEAN_BUILD" -eq 1 ]]; then
215	    if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/build" ]]; then
216	      echo "Cleaning previous build directory: $REPO_ROOT/build"
217	      rm -rf "$REPO_ROOT/build"
218	    fi
219	  else
220	    echo "Skipping clean of $REPO_ROOT/build (requested by --no-clean)"
221	  fi
222	
223	  if [ "$MANUAL_MODE" = "0" ]; then
224	  # Auto-detection mode
225	  echo "=========================================="
226	  echo "Auto-detecting CPU capabilities..."
227	  echo "=========================================="
228	  echo ""
229

231	  CPU_FEATURES=$(detect_cpu_features)
232	  HAS_AMX=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f1)
233	  HAS_AVX512F=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f2)
234	  HAS_AVX512_VNNI=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f3)
235	  HAS_AVX512_BF16=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f4)
236	  HAS_AVX512_VBMI=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f5)
237	
238	  export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=NATIVE
239	
240	  if [ "$HAS_AMX" = "1" ]; then
241	    echo "✓ AMX instructions detected"
242	    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=ON
243	    echo ""
244	    echo "Configuration: NATIVE + AMX=ON"
245	    echo "  ✓ Best performance on this machine"
246	    echo "  ✗ Binary requires Sapphire Rapids or newer CPU"
247	  else
248	    echo "ℹ AMX instructions not detected"
249	    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
250	    echo ""
251	    echo "Configuration: NATIVE + AMX=OFF"
252	    echo "  ✓ Using AVX512/AVX2 instructions"
253	  fi
254	
255	  echo ""
256	  echo "  ⚠️  IMPORTANT: This binary is optimized for THIS CPU only"
257	  echo "     To build portable binaries for distribution, use:"
258	  echo "       export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512  # or AVX2"
259	  echo "       export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF"
260	  echo "       ./install.sh build --manual"
261	
262	  # Fine-grained AVX512 subset detection (with fallback support)
263	  echo ""
264	  echo "AVX512 Feature Detection:"
265	
266	  # AVX512F: Foundation (required for all AVX512 variants)
267	  if [ "$HAS_AVX512F" = "1" ]; then
268	    echo "  AVX512F: ✓ Detected (foundation)"
269	  else
270	    echo "  AVX512F: ✗ Not detected (AVX512 not available)"
271	  fi
272	
273	  # VNNI: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
274	  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI:-}" ]; then
275	    echo "  VNNI: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI"
276	  else
277	    if [ "$HAS_AVX512_VNNI" = "1" ]; then
278	      echo "  VNNI: ✓ Detected (hardware acceleration enabled)"
279	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=ON
280	    else
281	      echo "  VNNI: ✗ Not detected (will use software fallback, 2-3x slower)"
282	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=OFF
283	    fi
284	  fi
285	
286	  # BF16: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
287	  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16:-}" ]; then
288	    echo "  BF16: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16"
289	  else

291	      echo "  BF16: ✓ Detected (hardware acceleration enabled)"
292	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=ON
293	    else
294	      echo "  BF16: ✗ Not detected (will use software fallback, 5-10x slower)"
295	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=OFF
296	    fi
297	  fi
298	
299	  # VBMI: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
300	  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI:-}" ]; then
301	    echo "  VBMI: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI"
302	  else
303	    if [ "$HAS_AVX512_VBMI" = "1" ]; then
304	      echo "  VBMI: ✓ Detected (byte permutation enabled)"
305	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=ON
306	    else
307	      echo "  VBMI: ✗ Not detected (FP8 MoE may not work)"
308	      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=OFF
309	    fi
310	  fi
311	
312	  echo ""
313	  echo "  Note: Software fallbacks ensure all code works on older CPUs"
314	  echo "  Note: FP8 MoE requires AVX512F + BF16 + VNNI + VBMI"
315	  echo "  Tip: Override with CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_[VNNI|BF16|VBMI]=ON/OFF"
316	
317	  echo ""

319	  echo ""

330	    NATIVE|FANCY|AVX512|AVX2)

334	      echo "Must be one of: NATIVE, FANCY, AVX512, AVX2"
335	      exit 1

337	  esac

340	  case "$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX" in
341	    ON|OFF)
342	      ;;
343	    *)
344	      echo "Error: Invalid CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX='$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX'"
345	      echo "Must be either: ON or OFF"
346	      exit 1
347	      ;;
348	  esac
349	
350	  # Warn about problematic configuration
351	  if [ "$CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT" = "NATIVE" ] && [ "$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX" = "OFF" ]; then
352	    CPU_FEATURES=$(detect_cpu_features)
353	    HAS_AMX=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f1)
354	    if [ "$HAS_AMX" = "1" ]; then
355	      echo "=========================================="
356	      echo "⚠️  WARNING: Risky Configuration"
357	      echo "=========================================="
358	      echo ""
359	      echo "Your configuration:"
360	      echo "  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT = NATIVE"
361	      echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX   = OFF"
362	      echo ""
363	      echo "Your CPU HAS AMX support!"
364	      echo ""
365	      echo "Problem:"
366	      echo "  • NATIVE uses -march=native which auto-enables ALL CPU features"
367	      echo "  • This may IGNORE your AMX=OFF setting"
368	      echo "  • The binary may still contain AMX instructions"
369	      echo ""
370	      echo "Recommended fixes:"
371	      echo "  1) For portable build (recommended for distribution):"
372	      echo "       export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512"
373	      echo ""
374	      echo "  2) If you want best performance on this CPU:"
375	      echo "       export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=ON"
376	      echo ""
377	      read -p "Continue with risky configuration? (y/N) " -n 1 -r
378	      echo
379	      if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
380	        exit 1
381	      fi
382	    fi
383	  fi
384	
385	# Close MANUAL_MODE conditional
386	  fi
387	
388	echo "=========================================="
389	echo "Building kt-kernel with configuration:"
390	echo "=========================================="
391	echo "  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT        = $CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT"
392	echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX          = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX"
393	echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI:-AUTO}"
394	echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16:-AUTO}"
395	echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI:-AUTO}"
396	echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE     = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE:-OFF}"
397	echo "  CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE          = ${CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE:-Release}"
398	echo "  CPUINFER_PARALLEL            = ${CPUINFER_PARALLEL:-AUTO}"
399	echo "  CPUINFER_VERBOSE             = ${CPUINFER_VERBOSE:-1}"
400	echo ""
401	
402	if [ ${CPUINFER_VERBOSE:-1} = "0" ]; then
403	  python3 -m pip install .
404	else
405	  python3 -m pip install . -v
406	fi
407	}
408	
409	# Subcommand dispatcher: default to "all"
410	SUBCMD="all"
411	if [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; then
412	  case "$1" in
413	    deps|build|all) SUBCMD="$1"; shift ;;
414	    -h|--help) usage ;;
415	    *) SUBCMD="build" ;; # backward compatibility: flags-only => build
416	  esac
417	fi
418	
419	case "$SUBCMD" in

421	    install_dependencies
422	    ;;
423	  build)
424	    build_step "$@"

427	    install_dependencies
428	    build_step "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
Usage: $0 [SUBCOMMAND] [BUILD_OPTIONS]

Two-step installation in one file. Choose a subcommand:

SUBCOMMANDS:
  deps            Install system prerequisites only
  build           Build and install kt-kernel (no dependency install)
  all             Run deps then build (default when no subcommand)
  -h, --help      Show this help message

BUILD_OPTIONS (for "build" or "all"):
  (none)          Auto-detect CPU and configure automatically (recommended)
  --manual        Skip auto-detection, use manual configuration (see below)
  --no-clean      Do not delete local build/ before building (default cleans)

AUTO-DETECTION (Default):
  The script will automatically detect your CPU and use ALL available features:
  - CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT = NATIVE (uses -march=native)
  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX   = ON/OFF (based on detection)
  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI = ON/OFF (with fallback if OFF)
  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16 = ON/OFF (with fallback if OFF)
  - CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI = ON/OFF (required for FP8 MoE)

  ✓ Best performance on YOUR machine
  ✗ Binary may NOT work on different/older CPUs

  Use this when: Installing for local use only

MANUAL CONFIGURATION:
  Use --manual flag when building for DISTRIBUTION or different machines.
  Set these environment variables before running:

  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT   - Target CPU instruction set
                            Options: AVX512, AVX2, FANCY, NATIVE
  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX     - Enable Intel AMX support
                            Options: ON, OFF

Distribution examples (portable binaries):

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Configuration          │ Target CPUs              │ Use Case             │
├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ AVX512 + AMX=OFF       │ Skylake-X, Ice Lake,     │ General distribution │
│                        │ Cascade Lake, Zen 4      │ (recommended)        │
├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ AVX2 + AMX=OFF         │ Haswell (2013) and newer │ Maximum compatibility│
├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ FANCY + AMX=OFF        │ Ice Lake+, Zen 4+        │ Modern CPUs only     │
│                        │ (with full AVX512 ext)   │                      │
└────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘

  Use this when: Building Docker images, PyPI packages, or deploying to clusters

  Example: Build for general distribution
    export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512
    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
    $0 build --manual
    # Result: Works on any CPU with AVX512 (2017+)

  Example: Build for maximum compatibility
    export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX2
    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
    $0 build --manual
    # Result: Works on any CPU with AVX2 (2013+)

Optional variables (with defaults):
  CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE=Release           Build type (Debug/RelWithDebInfo/Release)
  CPUINFER_PARALLEL=8                   Number of parallel build jobs
  CPUINFER_VERBOSE=1                    Verbose build output (0/1)
  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=ON/OFF    Override VNNI detection (auto if unset)
  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=ON/OFF    Override BF16 detection (auto if unset)
  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=ON/OFF    Override VBMI detection (auto if unset)
  CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE=ON/OFF       Enable native crash tracing (default OFF)

Software Fallback Support:
  ✓ If VNNI not available: Uses AVX512BW fallback (2-3x slower but works)
  ✓ If BF16 not available: Uses AVX512F fallback (5-10x slower but works)
  → Old CPUs with only AVX512F+BW can run all code (slower but functional)

EOF
  exit 1
}

install_dependencies() {
  echo "Checking and installing system dependencies..."

  # Determine if we need to use sudo
  SUDO=""
  if [ "${EUID:-0}" -ne 0 ]; then
    if command -v sudo &> /dev/null; then
      SUDO="sudo"
    else
      echo "Warning: Not running as root and sudo not found. Package installation may fail."
      echo "Please run as root or install sudo."
    fi
  fi

  if command -v conda &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing cmake via conda..."
    conda install -y cmake
  else
    echo "Warning: conda not found. Skipping cmake installation via conda."
    echo "Please install conda or manually install cmake."
  fi

  # Detect OS type
  if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then
    . /etc/os-release
    OS=$ID
  elif [ -f /etc/debian_version ]; then
    OS="debian"
  elif [ -f /etc/redhat-release ]; then
    OS="rhel"
  else
    echo "Warning: Unable to detect OS type. Skipping dependency installation."
    return 0
  fi

  # Install dependencies based on OS
  case "$OS" in
    debian|ubuntu|linuxmint|pop)
      echo "Detected Debian-based system. Installing libhwloc-dev and pkg-config..."
      $SUDO apt update
      $SUDO apt install -y libhwloc-dev pkg-config
      ;;
    fedora|rhel|centos|rocky|almalinux)
      echo "Detected Red Hat-based system. Installing hwloc-devel and pkgconfig..."
      $SUDO dnf install -y hwloc-devel pkgconfig || $SUDO yum install -y hwloc-devel pkgconfig
      ;;
    arch|manjaro)
      echo "Detected Arch-based system. Installing hwloc and pkgconf..."
      $SUDO pacman -S --noconfirm hwloc pkgconf
      ;;
    opensuse*|sles)
      echo "Detected openSUSE-based system. Installing hwloc-devel and pkg-config..."
      $SUDO zypper install -y hwloc-devel pkg-config
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Warning: Unsupported OS '$OS'. Please manually install libhwloc-dev and pkg-config."
      ;;
  esac
}

# Function to detect CPU features
# Returns: "has_amx has_avx512f has_avx512_vnni has_avx512_bf16 has_avx512_vbmi" (space-separated 0/1 values)
detect_cpu_features() {
  local has_amx=0
  local has_avx512f=0
  local has_avx512_vnni=0
  local has_avx512_bf16=0
  local has_avx512_vbmi=0

  if [ -f /proc/cpuinfo ]; then
    local cpu_flags
    cpu_flags=$(grep -m1 "^flags" /proc/cpuinfo | tr ' ' '\n')

    # Check for AMX support on Linux
    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "amx_tile|amx_int8|amx_bf16"; then
      has_amx=1
    fi

    # Check for AVX512F (foundation)
    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512f"; then
      has_avx512f=1
    fi

    # Check for AVX512_VNNI support
    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_vnni|avx512vnni"; then
      has_avx512_vnni=1
    fi

    # Check for AVX512_BF16 support
    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_bf16|avx512bf16"; then
      has_avx512_bf16=1
    fi

    # Check for AVX512_VBMI support
    if echo "$cpu_flags" | grep -qE "avx512_vbmi|avx512vbmi"; then
      has_avx512_vbmi=1
    fi
  elif [ "$(uname)" = "Darwin" ]; then
    # macOS doesn't have AMX (ARM or Intel without AMX)
    has_amx=0
    has_avx512f=0
    has_avx512_vnni=0
    has_avx512_bf16=0
    has_avx512_vbmi=0
  fi

  echo "$has_amx $has_avx512f $has_avx512_vnni $has_avx512_bf16 $has_avx512_vbmi"
}

build_step() {
  # Parse build-only flags from arguments to this function
  local MANUAL_MODE=0
  local CLEAN_BUILD=1
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --manual) MANUAL_MODE=1; shift ;;
      --no-clean) CLEAN_BUILD=0; shift ;;
      -h|--help) usage ;;
      *) break ;;
    esac
  done

  # Clean local build directory to ensure a fresh CMake/configure
  local REPO_ROOT
  REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  if [[ "$CLEAN_BUILD" -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/build" ]]; then
      echo "Cleaning previous build directory: $REPO_ROOT/build"
      rm -rf "$REPO_ROOT/build"
    fi
  else
    echo "Skipping clean of $REPO_ROOT/build (requested by --no-clean)"
  fi

  if [ "$MANUAL_MODE" = "0" ]; then
  # Auto-detection mode
  echo "=========================================="
  echo "Auto-detecting CPU capabilities..."
  echo "=========================================="
  echo ""

  # detect_cpu_features returns "has_amx has_avx512f has_avx512_vnni has_avx512_bf16 has_avx512_vbmi"
  CPU_FEATURES=$(detect_cpu_features)
  HAS_AMX=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f1)
  HAS_AVX512F=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f2)
  HAS_AVX512_VNNI=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f3)
  HAS_AVX512_BF16=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f4)
  HAS_AVX512_VBMI=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f5)

  export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=NATIVE

  if [ "$HAS_AMX" = "1" ]; then
    echo "✓ AMX instructions detected"
    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=ON
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration: NATIVE + AMX=ON"
    echo "  ✓ Best performance on this machine"
    echo "  ✗ Binary requires Sapphire Rapids or newer CPU"
  else
    echo "ℹ AMX instructions not detected"
    export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration: NATIVE + AMX=OFF"
    echo "  ✓ Using AVX512/AVX2 instructions"
  fi

  echo ""
  echo "  ⚠️  IMPORTANT: This binary is optimized for THIS CPU only"
  echo "     To build portable binaries for distribution, use:"
  echo "       export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512  # or AVX2"
  echo "       export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=OFF"
  echo "       ./install.sh build --manual"

  # Fine-grained AVX512 subset detection (with fallback support)
  echo ""
  echo "AVX512 Feature Detection:"

  # AVX512F: Foundation (required for all AVX512 variants)
  if [ "$HAS_AVX512F" = "1" ]; then
    echo "  AVX512F: ✓ Detected (foundation)"
  else
    echo "  AVX512F: ✗ Not detected (AVX512 not available)"
  fi

  # VNNI: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI:-}" ]; then
    echo "  VNNI: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI"
  else
    if [ "$HAS_AVX512_VNNI" = "1" ]; then
      echo "  VNNI: ✓ Detected (hardware acceleration enabled)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=ON
    else
      echo "  VNNI: ✗ Not detected (will use software fallback, 2-3x slower)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI=OFF
    fi
  fi

  # BF16: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16:-}" ]; then
    echo "  BF16: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16"
  else
    if [ "$HAS_AVX512_BF16" = "1" ]; then
      echo "  BF16: ✓ Detected (hardware acceleration enabled)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=ON
    else
      echo "  BF16: ✗ Not detected (will use software fallback, 5-10x slower)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16=OFF
    fi
  fi

  # VBMI: Check if user manually set it, otherwise auto-detect
  if [ -n "${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI:-}" ]; then
    echo "  VBMI: User override = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI"
  else
    if [ "$HAS_AVX512_VBMI" = "1" ]; then
      echo "  VBMI: ✓ Detected (byte permutation enabled)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=ON
    else
      echo "  VBMI: ✗ Not detected (FP8 MoE may not work)"
      export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI=OFF
    fi
  fi

  echo ""
  echo "  Note: Software fallbacks ensure all code works on older CPUs"
  echo "  Note: FP8 MoE requires AVX512F + BF16 + VNNI + VBMI"
  echo "  Tip: Override with CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_[VNNI|BF16|VBMI]=ON/OFF"

  echo ""
  echo "To use manual configuration instead, run: $0 build --manual"
  echo ""
  else
  # Manual mode - validate user configuration (no exports)
  if [ -z "$CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT" ] || [ -z "$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX" ]; then
    echo "Error: Manual mode requires CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT and CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX to be set."
    echo ""
    usage
  fi

  # Validate CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT
  case "$CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT" in
    NATIVE|FANCY|AVX512|AVX2)
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Error: Invalid CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT='$CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT'"
      echo "Must be one of: NATIVE, FANCY, AVX512, AVX2"
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac

  # Validate CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX
  case "$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX" in
    ON|OFF)
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Error: Invalid CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX='$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX'"
      echo "Must be either: ON or OFF"
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac

  # Warn about problematic configuration
  if [ "$CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT" = "NATIVE" ] && [ "$CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX" = "OFF" ]; then
    CPU_FEATURES=$(detect_cpu_features)
    HAS_AMX=$(echo "$CPU_FEATURES" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [ "$HAS_AMX" = "1" ]; then
      echo "=========================================="
      echo "⚠️  WARNING: Risky Configuration"
      echo "=========================================="
      echo ""
      echo "Your configuration:"
      echo "  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT = NATIVE"
      echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX   = OFF"
      echo ""
      echo "Your CPU HAS AMX support!"
      echo ""
      echo "Problem:"
      echo "  • NATIVE uses -march=native which auto-enables ALL CPU features"
      echo "  • This may IGNORE your AMX=OFF setting"
      echo "  • The binary may still contain AMX instructions"
      echo ""
      echo "Recommended fixes:"
      echo "  1) For portable build (recommended for distribution):"
      echo "       export CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT=AVX512"
      echo ""
      echo "  2) If you want best performance on this CPU:"
      echo "       export CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX=ON"
      echo ""
      read -p "Continue with risky configuration? (y/N) " -n 1 -r
      echo
      if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        exit 1
      fi
    fi
  fi

# Close MANUAL_MODE conditional
  fi

echo "=========================================="
echo "Building kt-kernel with configuration:"
echo "=========================================="
echo "  CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT        = $CPUINFER_CPU_INSTRUCT"
echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX          = $CPUINFER_ENABLE_AMX"
echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VNNI:-AUTO}"
echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_BF16:-AUTO}"
echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI  = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_AVX512_VBMI:-AUTO}"
echo "  CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE     = ${CPUINFER_ENABLE_CPPTRACE:-OFF}"
echo "  CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE          = ${CPUINFER_BUILD_TYPE:-Release}"
echo "  CPUINFER_PARALLEL            = ${CPUINFER_PARALLEL:-AUTO}"
echo "  CPUINFER_VERBOSE             = ${CPUINFER_VERBOSE:-1}"
echo ""

if [ ${CPUINFER_VERBOSE:-1} = "0" ]; then
  python3 -m pip install .
else
  python3 -m pip install . -v
fi
}

# Subcommand dispatcher: default to "all"
SUBCMD="all"
if [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; then
  case "$1" in
    deps|build|all) SUBCMD="$1"; shift ;;
    -h|--help) usage ;;
    *) SUBCMD="build" ;; # backward compatibility: flags-only => build
  esac
fi

case "$SUBCMD" in
  deps)
    install_dependencies
    ;;
  build)
    build_step "$@"
    ;;
  all)
    install_dependencies
    build_step "$@"
    ;;
esac
